items.title: Oil up almost 3% as US-Iran peace talks stall
items.desc: Oil prices jumped almost 3% on Monday as ​peace talks between the ​U.S. and Iran stalled and shipments through the Strait of ​Hormuz remained limited, keeping global oil supplies tight.

items.title: Iran FM blames US for failure of talks after landing in Russia
items.desc: Iran’s top diplomat blamed Washington on Monday for the failure of talks after landing in Russia as part of a whirlwind diplomatic tour, with direct negotiations between the warring parties seemingly at an impasse. Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi made the rem…

items.title: Iran offers to end chokehold on Strait of Hormuz, asks US to end blockade, officials say
items.desc: Iran proposes lifting its Strait of Hormuz blockade. This offer excludes addressing its nuclear program. Iran also seeks an end to the US blockade on its country. Meanwhile, Iran's Foreign Minister is in Russia for talks on the ongoing war. Tensions persist i…

items.title: Russian drone attack on Odesa wounds 10, officials say
items.desc: An overnight Russian drone attack on Ukraine's southern city of ⁠Odesa wounded 10 people, including two children, and damaged residential buildings, Ukrainian officials said today.

items.title: A quiet and fatal threat: How the drone became a greater danger than anti-tank missiles
items.desc: The serious incident in al-Tayiba, in which Sgt. Idan Fooks was killed, reveals Hezbollah’s new weapon: explosive drones guided via fiber-optic cable.

items.title: Marvel Comics Reveals Final Queen In Black Covers, Including Swimsuit And Knullbuster Variants
items.desc: Marvel Comics has revealed the final Queen in Black variant covers, along with special "Knullbuster Variant Covers" that will adorn various titles as the event plays out over the summer.

items.title: CNA Explains: What is UNCLOS, the international law in the spotlight amid the Iran conflict?
items.desc: Known as the constitution for the oceans, UNCLOS is a set of maritime rules governing everything from coastal boundaries, environmental protection, and the right for ships to transit through territorial waters.

items.title: SSE, TotalEnergies UK Wind Farm Delays Contract That Would Cap Profits
items.desc: One of the UK’s largest offshore wind farms delayed starting a government contract that would cap the price of its electricity, instead choosing to sell power in the market at more profitable rates.

items.title: Araghchi arrives in Russia for close consultations on regional, international issues
items.desc: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has arrived in Russia to continue close consultations between Tehran and Moscow on regional and international issues, and to enhance bilateral relations.

items.title: Securing advanced manufacturing in Bendigo with new Bushmasters
items.desc: The Albanese Government is investing $750 million to deliver a pipeline of Bushmasters for the Australian Defence Force's (ADF) fleet of protected mobility vehicles in regional Victoria, supporting around 300 local jobs for the next seven years.

items.title: Former Israeli PMs join forces to dethrone Netanyahu in upcoming elections
items.desc: Two former Israeli prime ministers have said they would join forces in upcoming elections expected later this year in a bid to dethrone longtime prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

items.title: Trump Says Tehran 'Can Call Us' If It Wants To Talk, While Iran's Araqchi Arrives In Russia
items.desc: US President Donald Trump said that Tehran "can call us" if it wants to talk, but he added that it must give up any plans to build a nuclear weapon or "there's no reason to meet."

items.title: Trump says he canceled U.S. representatives' trip to Pakistan for talks with Iran
items.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump announced on social media Saturday that he just canceled the trip of U.S. representatives going to Islamabad, Pakistan, to meet with the Iranian side.
